Málaga → Bucharest · 25–30 June

Carpathian
Nature Loop

A anticlockwise road trip for two drivers — three castles, two legendary mountain roads, and two nights in forest cabins. Bucharest up to Sinaia, across to the Piatra Craiului wilderness, over the Transfăgărășan, then the Transalpina home.

5 nights ~1,100 km 2 scenic roads 3 castles 1 national park 2 cabin nights

Meet the crew

Two Great Drivers

One Romanian road loop, two adventure addicts, endless playlists, mountain curves, snack stops, and the kind of chaos that turns every kilometer into a story.

Driver 1

Amanda

Adventure Seeker · Budget Manager · Navigator · Memory Maker

Keeps the adventure organized, the budget under control, and the road-trip energy at full power from the very first kilometer to the last mountain view.

Driver 2

Altaf

Scenic Route Hunter · Off-Roader · Playlist Curator · Snack Supplier

Finds the best roads, chases the highest mountain passes, and somehow makes every scenic detour feel like the highlight of the journey.

Fueled by coffee, powered by playlists, and ready for legendary mountain roads.

The path we trace

How We Cover the Loop

The whole anticlockwise circuit — ~1,100 km over five driving days, colour-coded so you can see exactly how the trip unfolds. Online it draws on the real roads; offline it shows as a clean schematic.

Thu · to Sinaia Fri · Castles Sat · Piatra Craiului Sun · Transfăgărășan Mon · Transalpina

Drag to pan · pinch or +/− to zoom · hover a coloured path for that day's details · tap a stop for its name

Drivers Thu · Arrival Fri · Castles Sat · Piatra Craiului Sun · Transfăgărășan Mon · Transalpina Tue · Home Route map Before you drive
01

Thursday · 25 June

Land late, drive to the mountains

Otopeni Airport → Sinaia 125 km ≈ 2 h Land 23:00 · car 00:00 · arrive ~02:00
Night 1 · Cabin

CHECK-IN ~02:00 · sleep in

Casa Oprea, Central Cozy Cottage

Sinaia, Județul Prahova

A warm, central cottage in Sinaia — close to everything yet quiet, an easy soft landing after the 2am drive. No alarm Friday; the castle waits.

cottageSinaia centre
Open in Maps Airbnb

Heads-up

  • The 2am arrival after a flight is the trip's one tiring stretch. Swap drivers for the mountain section of the DN1, take it slow — there will be very little traffic at that hour.
  • Fuel up at the airport before leaving; nothing useful is open en route at midnight.
02

Friday · 26 June

Castles, then into the forest

Sinaia → Brașov → Bran ~170 km ≈ 3.5 h driving
Castle · book ahead

09:30 · ~1.5 h

Peleș Castle

Aleea Peleșului 2, Sinaia

Fairytale Neo-Renaissance castle — the first in Europe fully powered by electricity. Visits are timed group tours only, so buy tickets online in advance. No on-site parking; park in town and walk up (~20 min) or grab a quick taxi.

★ 4.7Open Fri 9:15–17:00closed Mon & Tue
Open in Maps Tickets
Old town

12:00 · ~2 h + lunch

Council Square, Brașov

Piața Sfatului, Brașov

A quick stretch of city before the wild: the medieval square, the Black Church, colourful merchant houses and the Brașov sign on Tâmpa. Plenty of terraces for lunch — eat here before heading into cabin country.

★ 4.8open 24h
Open in Maps
Castle · busy pm

15:00 · ~1 h

Bran Castle

Str. General Traian Moșoiu 24, Bran

The "Dracula" castle — striking from outside, though the Dracula link is mostly marketing and the interior is compact. Busiest mid-afternoon; pre-book a slot to skip the queue. The real Vlad fortress comes Sunday.

★ 4.3Open 9:00–19:00
Open in Maps Tickets
Nights 2 & 3 · Cabin

ARRIVE ~17:30 · two nights

The GuestHouse, Bran

Bran, Brașov County

A 4-star guesthouse on the edge of Bran, right under the mountains — garden, mountain views, and a comfortable base for both castle country and the Piatra Craiului day. Two relaxed nights here.

★★★★guesthouseBran
Open in Maps TripAdvisor

Heads-up

  • Optional Măgura detour: the scattered village above Bran has some of the best Piatra Craiului views — worth a slow afternoon drive up if the weather is clear (last stretch is partly gravel).
  • Stock a few snacks/drinks in Bran or Zărnești before going up; the village shop is tiny.
03

Saturday · 27 June

A full day in the wild

Around Piatra Craiului · back to the cabin ~70 km ≈ 1.5 h driving
Optional · book ahead

09:15 slot · ~1.5 h

Libearty Bear Sanctuary

Zărnești (last 2 km gravel)

Europe's largest brown-bear sanctuary — rescued bears roaming oak forest. Entry is by timed morning slot only, so book online the day before. A steep, hour-long walking tour; wear proper shoes.

★ 4.6Tue–Sun 9:15–11:15closed Mon
Open in Maps Book
National park

Midday onward · at your pace

Piatra Craiului National Park

Trailheads at Zărnești / Fântâna lui Botorog

The day's real point: hike the limestone ridge trails, walk the Zărnești Gorge, find a river and a viewpoint. A well-marked classic runs from Fântâna lui Botorog up to Cabana Curmătura. Then back to the cabin for a campfire evening.

★ 4.8hiking / forest trails
Open in Maps

Heads-up

  • This is your rest-and-nature day on purpose — no long drive, no fixed schedule. Bank the energy for the two big mountain days that follow.
  • Bear country: carry water, make noise on quiet trails, and don't leave food out at the cabin.
04

Sunday · 28 June

The Transfăgărășan

Bran → Curtea de Argeș → Poenari → Bâlea → Porumbacu ~270 km ≈ 6 h + stops Leave early · road open 07:00–21:00
The REAL Dracula castle

10:00 · ~1 h (the climb)

Poenari Citadel

Transfăgărășan, Arefu

Vlad the Impaler's actual fortress, clinging to a crag at the foot of the road. The catch: ~1,480 steps straight up (benches along the way). Worth it for the history and the valley views. May close on short notice for bear activity.

★ 4.5Open 10:00–18:00last entry ~15:00
Open in Maps Info
Dam + lake

11:30 · quick stop

Lake Vidraru & Dam

Transfăgărășan (southern climb)

A turquoise reservoir behind a 166 m concrete arch — the gateway to the high road. Cross the dam, take in the view, then start the serpentine climb toward the peaks.

★ 4.7lake / viewpoint
Open in Maps
Highlight · 2,034 m

14:00 · the top

Bâlea Lake

Transfăgărășan, Cârțișoara

The glacial lake at the crown of the road, ringed by peaks. Park, walk the shore, grab a hot trout and a coffee at the chalet. This is the photo you came for — then the famous hairpin descent to the north.

top of the Transfăgărășanchalet + food
Open in Maps
Night 4 · To be decided

ARRIVE ~16:30

Cabin near the Transfăgărășan — TBD

Porumbacu / Cârțișoara area (still to book)

Still deciding on this one. Aim for somewhere at the northern foot of the Transfăgărășan — forest quiet, ~40 min from Sibiu — so you roll over the pass early the next morning before the crowds.

not booked yetforest cabin

Heads-up

  • Start early. Sunday is a summer weekend — Bâlea gets packed by midday. Rolling over the top before the crowds is the whole game.
  • The road reopened for the season on 12 June; it runs 07:00–21:00 daily. Bears appear roadside — do not stop to feed or approach them (it's illegal and fined heavily).
  • Fuel before the climb. Weather flips fast at 2,000 m — keep a layer and a rain shell handy.
05

Monday · 29 June

The Transalpina, the long way home

Sibiu → Sebeș → Urdele → Rânca → Novaci → Cozia → Bucharest ~465 km ≈ 8–9 h + stops Ready 08:00 · arrive ~18:45
Quick start

08:00 · leave

Sibiu

Old town centre

Grab a coffee and a look at the Saxon old town on the way out — then west on the A1 toward Sebeș. Don't linger; the mountain is the morning.

Open in Maps
FUEL UP HERE

08:45 · 15 min

Sebeș

Northern foot of the Transalpina

Fill the tank here. There's no reliable fuel on the high section. Last proper coffee and snack stop before the climb begins.

Open in Maps
Highest pass · 2,145 m

11:00 · 45 min

Pasul Urdele

Transalpina (DN67C) summit

The roof of the whole trip — the highest road pass in Romania, higher than anything on the Transfăgărășan. Park, walk a little, take it all in. The 30 km/h limit on this stretch just gives you more time to look.

★ 4.92,145 m
Open in Maps
Lunch

12:30 · ~75 min

Rânca

Mountain resort below the passes

Best lunch of the day — terraces with traditional food (ciorbă, grilled meats) and big Transalpina views. Eat well here; after Novaci it's transit, not scenery.

★ 4.4resort / restaurants
Open in Maps
Olt Valley breather

16:15 · 20–30 min

Cozia Monastery

Calea lui Traian 932, Căciulata

Right on the road in the Olt gorge — a 14th-century monastery with painted walls and river views behind. A calm stretch-and-breathe stop that breaks up the run-in. (Main church may be under renovation; the chapel and grounds still deliver.)

★ 4.7Open 8:00–21:00
Open in Maps
Night 5 · City

ARRIVE ~18:45

Bucharest apartment

Bucharest

A bright, comfortable apartment as your final base. Drop the car, then dinner and drinks out — you land after 6pm exactly as planned, with both legendary roads behind you. The Old Town is a short hop away on foot or by cab.

apartmentnightlife / restaurants
Open in Maps Airbnb

Heads-up — the long day

  • Transalpina night closure: the high section is shut 20:00–09:00 through 30 June. Your 08:00 start puts you at the gate right as it opens at 09:00 — perfect timing.
  • This is your second alpine day in a row. The morning is glorious; the afternoon Olt-valley transit is the tired part — swap drivers after Novaci.
  • Bad weather on the passes? Bail out: skip the crossing and take the A1 straight Sibiu→Bucharest (~3.5 h) for a relaxed afternoon in the city instead.
06

Tuesday · 30 June

Slow morning, fly home

Bucharest → Otopeni Airport ~20 km car back 12:00 flight 14:00

Wind down

  • Relaxed breakfast, then a short drive to the airport. Leave a buffer for the rental return and refuel near Otopeni before dropping the car.
  • That's the loop closed: ~1,100 km, three castles, two of Europe's best driving roads, and two nights under the stars. Drum bun.

Before you drive

The handful of things that make or break this specific route.

The car

Skip the cheapest economy box. A Golf, Octavia or Focus-class car makes the mountain roads a joy, not a chore — and handles any gravel side-roads comfortably.

Book ahead

Peleș (timed tickets, closed Mon/Tue), Bran (slot to skip queues), and the Libearty bear sanctuary (morning slots only). Do these online the day before.

Mountain road hours

Transfăgărășan: 07:00–21:00 daily. Transalpina high section: shut 20:00–09:00 through 30 June, with a 30 km/h limit on the reopened stretch. Plan starts around them.

Fuel discipline

Top up before both scenic roads — at Curtea de Argeș / Vidraru for the Transfăgărășan, and at Sebeș for the Transalpina. No reliable stations up top.

Drive in shifts

Sunday and Monday are back-to-back alpine days. Let whoever isn't driving the scenic stretch take the transit legs. Rotate; stay fresh.

Bears & weather

This is Europe's densest bear range — never stop to approach or feed them. At 2,000 m the weather turns in minutes: pack layers and a rain shell even in June.

Romania Nature Loop · 25–30 June Málaga → Bucharest · 2 drivers · ~1,100 km
